title: Viking Cruises Antarctica Cruises 2026
intro: Viking Cruises brings its signature Scandinavian elegance to the White Continent with intimate, expedition-focused voyages aboard purpose-built polar vessels. Unlike traditional cruise lines, Viking's Antarctica program emphasizes education and enrichment, with a resident scientific staff leading lectures on glaciology, marine biology, and climate research. The all-veranda ship design ensures every guest witnesses calving glaciers and breaching humpbacks from private observation spaces. Viking's inclusive model covers virtually all expenses, eliminating the nickel-and-diming common on competitor lines. With just 378 guests maximum, these sailings foster genuine connection among like-minded explorers while maintaining the spaciousness of a boutique hotel. The line's commitment to sustainability—carbon-neutral operations and single-use plastic elimination—resonates deeply with environmentally conscious travelers seeking meaningful polar exploration without compromising comfort or conscience.

faqs.question: How much does a Viking Cruises Antarctica cruise cost?
faqs.answer: Viking Antarctica cruises start at €13,490 per person for 13-night voyages in standard Nordic Balcony staterooms during early season, rising to €16,490+ for peak December-January departures. Explorer Suites command €24,990-€29,990. Unlike competitors, Viking's pricing includes virtually everything: premium beverages, gratuities, WiFi, all excursions, and expedition gear. The only significant extras are business-class flights and pre-cruise hotels. When comparing total vacation cost against premium competitors, Viking often proves competitively priced despite higher upfront fares due to comprehensive inclusions.

faqs.question: Which Viking Cruises ships sail to Antarctica?
faqs.answer: Viking deploys its identical polar-class expedition vessels Viking Octantis and Viking Polaris to Antarctica, both launched in 2022 specifically for extreme latitudes. These 378-guest ships feature ice-strengthened hulls rated PC6, submarine-operating capabilities, and The Hangar—an industry-first enclosed marina for seamless Zodiac boarding. Unlike Viking's ocean fleet, these expedition ships carry kayaks, snowshoes, and two submarines (additional fee). The Nordic design aesthetic, finnish sauna, and Aquavit Terrace remain consistent with Viking's brand, while scientific laboratories and expedition briefing theaters distinguish these vessels.

faqs.question: When is best time to cruise Antarctica?
faqs.answer: Viking's Antarctic season runs November through February, with each month offering distinct experiences. November delivers pristine snowscapes, dramatic ice formations, and courting penguin rituals—ideal for photographers seeking untouched wilderness. December-January represents peak season: 20+ hours of daylight, hatching penguin chicks, and optimal wildlife activity justify premium pricing. February brings whale migration peaks, accessible landing sites as ice recedes, and softer light for photography. Viking operates primarily December-January for reliability, though early and late season sailings offer savings and solitude. Weather windows remain unpredictable regardless of month.

faqs.question: What is included?
faqs.answer: Viking's Antarctica cruises are comprehensively inclusive: all meals across multiple venues including Manfredi's Italian and The Restaurant; beer, wine, and soft drinks with lunch and dinner; one included shore excursion per port; unlimited Zodiac excursions and guided landings in Antarctica; daily lectures by resident scientists; complimentary WiFi; all gratuities; airport transfers; and a Viking expedition jacket to keep. Kayaking, snowshoeing, and photography workshops are included. Not covered: premium spirits outside mealtimes, spa treatments, submarine dives, helicopter excursions, and international airfare—though Viking offers competitive air packages.

faqs.question: Which ports?
faqs.answer: Viking Antarctica itineraries center on Ushuaia, Argentina—the world's southernmost city—as primary embarkation point, with some sailings offering Buenos Aires departures including pre-cruise hotel stays. The Antarctic Peninsula itself has no formal ports; instead, expedition leaders select landing sites daily based on ice, weather, and wildlife conditions, potentially including Neko Harbour, Paradise Bay, or Deception Island. Extended itineraries incorporate Patagonian calls at Puerto Madryn for penguin colonies, and scenic cruising of Cape Horn and Chilean fjords. Each sailing includes multiple continental landings subject to IAATO regulations limiting site visits.

faqs.question: How far ahead to book?
faqs.answer: Viking Antarctica cruises require booking 12-18 months in advance for optimal cabin selection, with most departures selling out 10-12 months ahead. Early booking incentives typically include 10-15% savings and reduced deposits. The small 378-guest capacity creates exceptional demand among Viking's loyal repeat clientele who receive priority booking windows. Waitlists operate for sold-out sailings, though cancellations are rare given the investment involved. For specific cabin categories—particularly Explorer Suites on higher decks—booking at initial release 18+ months ahead is essential. Final payment is typically due 120 days before departure.

faqs.question: Good for families?
faqs.answer: Viking's Antarctica cruises are designed exclusively for adults aged 18 and older, with no children's programming, facilities, or discounted fares. The expedition focus, scientific lectures, and extended sea days through the Drake Passage demand maturity and attention spans beyond most younger travelers. The educational enrichment, documentary screenings, and evening cultural programming target intellectually curious adults. For families seeking polar exploration, competitors like Hurtigruten and Quark Expeditions offer dedicated family departures with age-appropriate activities. Viking's adult-only policy ensures a serene, contemplative atmosphere appropriate for the profound wilderness experience Antarctica represents.

faqs.question: Cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Viking's Antarctica cancellation policy reflects the expedition nature and high demand: deposits are fully refundable for 7 days after booking, then non-refundable. Cancellation 121+ days before departure forfeits deposit; 120-91 days costs 25% of fare; 90-61 days costs 50%; 60-31 days costs 75%; and 30 days or less forfeits 100%. Comprehensive travel insurance is strongly recommended given the substantial investment and remote destination—Viking offers its own protection plan covering cancellation for any reason up to 75% credit, plus medical evacuation coverage essential for Antarctic travel. Weather-related itinerary changes do not qualify for compensation given the expedition nature.
